turnaround_reason: Stock cratered ~63% from $34 highs after a Q2 2026 revenue miss (-9% YoY), $249M impairment charge, and guidance cut; CEO transition and GLP-1 headwinds crushed sentiment. Insiders (director Kilts, ex-CEO of ConAgra, bought $991K at $12.39) are stepping in at distressed valuations.
bullish_points: Insider buying by seasoned CPG veteran director James Kilts ($991K at $12.39) signals conviction from someone who knows turnarounds
Trading at 0.77x P/B and ~7.1x forward PE — deeply cheap if revenue stabilizes
EV/EBITDA of 6.3x on an operating-margin business still generating $173M OCF and $125M FCF
Current ratio of 5.1x provides ample liquidity; debt/eq of 30 is manageable
Two consecutive earnings beats (8.2% and 13.6% surprise) show cost control is working even as top line shrinks
bearish_points: Revenue declining 9% YoY — structural headwind from GLP-1 weight loss drugs cannibalizing diet/snack categories
Large $249M impairment charge signals management admits prior growth assumptions were wrong
FY26 guidance: net sales -2% to +2%, adjusted EBITDA -4% to +1% — no real growth expected
Gross margins declining 100-150bps due to commodity inflation and tariffs
No analyst consensus buy rating; low institutional conviction (recommendation: 'none')
catalyst: Q3 2026 earnings on July 9 — if revenue decline stabilizes and margins bottom, the stock could re-rate from distressed multiples
suggested_trade: Spot only, small size. Avoid options — only 3 expiries and thin OI. Wait for Q3 earnings to confirm revenue stabilization before adding.
key_risk: GLP-1 adoption is a structural headwind that could permanently shrink the addressable market for diet/snack products, making SMPL a value trap rather than a turnaround
would_skip_if: Q3 earnings shows further revenue acceleration to the downside OR gross margins deteriorate beyond guidance
